summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identity to hardcoded IP at install time
details: On `pip install`, setup.py executes a `_beacon()` function before `setup()` that collects host identity (`socket.gethostname()`, `subprocess.getoutput('id')`, `subprocess.getoutput('uname -a')`, cwd, Python version) and sends it via HTTP GET to `http://157.173.126.113:8888/depconf-rce` with the data URL-encoded in the query string. The package is named `inversiones-common` and published to public PyPI at version `99.0.0`, an unusually high version number characteristic of dependency-confusion attacks designed to outrank an internal package of the same name in misconfigured private-index resolutions. The setup.py source self-describes as a `FINTUAL-DEPCONF-RCE` proof-of-concept and labels the network call 'the RCE primitive', but executes unconditionally on any installer that resolves this package — regardless of whether that installer is the intended target. Any developer or CI environment that pulls this name will leak host identifiers to the hardcoded IP, and the same install-time network primitive can be repurposed to fetch and execute arbitrary remote payloads.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
